issue_title: IntentGate misroutes explicit `open`/`tap` phrases when target text contains a navigation keyword (back/home/scroll)
issue_body:
### What happened?

`IntentGate.detectExactCommand` ([IntentGate.kt:112-158](https://github.com/touchpilot/touchpilot/blob/201dfb32b14b314a2024a8471e3ebb2a4119124d/^Gpp/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/IntentGate.kt#L112-L158)) checks the bare navigation-keyword substrings `"back"`, `"home"`, `"scroll"`, and `"scroll up"` **before** the explicit-phrase regexes `OpenAppPattern` and `TapPattern`:

```kotlin
private fun detectExactCommand(normalized: String): IntentDecision.ExactCommand? {
    if ("scroll up" in normalized) { /* scroll backward */ }
    if ("scroll" in normalized)    { /* scroll forward  */ }
    if ("back" in normalized)      { /* press_back      */ }
    if ("home" in normalized)      { /* press_home      */ }
    OpenAppPattern.find(normalized)?...?.let { return open_app }
    TapPattern.find(normalized)?...?.let { return tap }
    return null
}
```

Two compounding problems:

**Problem 1 â€” explicit `open`/`tap` phrases lose to bare keywords inside their own target.**
Any user task whose target text contains a navigation keyword is misrouted because the bare substring check fires first:

| User task              | Expected route                       | Actual route on `main`                   |
|------------------------|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `open back button`     | `open_app(target="back button")`     | `press_back` (back is taken from target) |
| `tap home shortcut`    | `tap(text="home shortcut")`          | `press_home`                             |
| `tap scroll bar`       | `tap(text="scroll bar")`             | `scroll(direction=forward)`              |
| `tap back`             | `tap(text="back")`                   | `press_back`                             |

The user typed the explicit verb (`open`/`tap`) and the explicit target â€” the deterministic route should honor it, not strip the target out and use it as a different command.

**Problem 2 â€” bare keywords have no word boundary, so they trigger on accidental substring hits.**
`"back" in normalized` matches inside `"feedback"`, `"comeback"`, `"playback"`. `"home" in normalized` matches inside `"homemade"`. `"scroll" in normalized` matches inside `"scrollbar"`. None of those are real navigation intents; all of them would short-circuit ahead of any meaningful classification.

The author already understands precedence in this file: `IntentGateTest.screenInquiryWinsOverExactCommandWhenBothCouldMatch` ([IntentGateTest.kt:169-174](https://github.com/touchpilot/touchpilot/blob/201dfb32b14b314a2024a8471e3ebb2a4119124d/^Gpp/src/test/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/IntentGateTest.kt#L169-L174)) explicitly pins that `"summarize the home screen"` is classified as a screen inquiry *because* it would otherwise trip `press_home`. The same precedence shape applies to the `open`/`tap` route â€” the test for `"summarize the home screen"` is the workaround that fenced one downstream of the gate, but the underlying ordering between explicit and bare keywords was never repaired.

### Expected behavior

- A user task whose explicit verb matches `OpenAppPattern` or `TapPattern` MUST be routed to `open_app` / `tap`, even when the target text happens to contain `back`, `home`, `scroll`, or `scroll up`.
- Bare navigation routes (`press_back`, `press_home`, `scroll`) MUST match the keyword as a **word**, not a substring. `"feedback"`, `"homemade"`, `"scrollbar"`, `"comeback"`, `"playback"` must not trigger them.
- All currently-passing `IntentGateTest` cases (Go back, go home, scroll up, scroll down, Open Settings, launch maps, tap OK, summarize the home screen, etc.) must remain green; this is a *precedence* and *word-boundary* fix, not a route-set change.

### Steps to reproduce

The bug is in deterministic intent classification, reproduced by JVM unit test.

1. From the touchpilot project root, add a temporary regression test at `app/src/test/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/IntentGateBugRepro.kt`:

   ```kotlin
   package dev.touchpilot.app.agent

   import kotlin.test.Test
   import kotlin.test.assertEquals
   import kotlin.test.assertIs

   class IntentGateBugRepro {
       private val gate = IntentGate()

       @Test
       fun openAppWithTargetContainingBackIsOpenApp() {
           val command = assertIs<IntentDecision.ExactCommand>(gate.classify("open back button"))
           assertEquals("open_app", command.tool)
           assertEquals("back button", command.args["target"])
       }

       @Test
       fun tapTargetContainingHomeIsTap() {
           val command = assertIs<IntentDecision.ExactCommand>(gate.classify("tap home shortcut"))
           assertEquals("tap", command.tool)
           assertEquals("home shortcut", command.args["text"])
       }

       @Test
       fun tapTargetContainingScrollIsTap() {
           val command = assertIs<IntentDecision.ExactCommand>(gate.classify("tap scroll bar"))
           assertEquals("tap", command.tool)
           assertEquals("scroll bar", command.args["text"])
       }
   }
   ```

2. Run only the new test class:

   ```
   .\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest --tests dev.touchpilot.app.agent.IntentGateBugRepro
   ```

3. Observe three failures on `main` (commit `201dfb3`):

   ```
   IntentGateBugRepro > openAppWithTargetContainingBackIsOpenApp FAILED
       AssertionError: Expected open_app, actual press_back ...

   IntentGateBugRepro > tapTargetContainingHomeIsTap FAILED
       AssertionError: Expected tap, actual press_home ...

   IntentGateBugRepro > tapTargetContainingScrollIsTap FAILED
       AssertionError: Expected tap, actual scroll ...

   3 tests completed, 3 failed
   ```

   Each failure attributes to the same precedence trap: `detectExactCommand` returned `ExactCommand` for `press_back` / `press_home` / `scroll` before `OpenAppPattern` / `TapPattern` had a chance to claim the input.

4. To independently observe the word-boundary half of the bug, classify `"give feedback"` or `"buy homemade pizza"` against the same gate; both will route to `press_back` / `press_home` on `main` even though neither task asks for navigation.

Delete the regression test file once observed; this issue does not propose committing it.

### Device or emulator

JVM (`./gradlew testDebugUnitTest`); no emulator required. The on-device behavior is observable from the chat input on any TouchPilot build â€” typing `tap back` is routed to `press_back` instead of `tap("back")` â€” but the unit-test repro is deterministic.

### Android version

Android 9 Pie, API level 28 (irrelevant for the JVM repro). Verified on LDPlayer 9 instance 0, 720Ã—1440 @ 240 dpi, Windows 10 Pro x64.

### TouchPilot commit

`201dfb32b14b314a2024a8471e3ebb2a4119124d`

### Accessibility service enabled?

Yes (irrelevant for the JVM repro).

### Areas involved

- [ ] Android control
- [x] Agent loop
- [ ] Local router / local inference
- [ ] Skills
- [ ] MCP
- [ ] Security / approvals
- [ ] UI

### Logs, screenshots, or traces

Genuine Gradle HTML test report from the JVM repro on `main` (commit `201dfb3`), captured from `app/build/reports/tests/testDebugUnitTest/` in a browser.

**Class report — all three repros fail; each one reports the wrong `ExactCommand` for an input whose target text contains a navigation keyword:**

![failing class report](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/galuis116/touchpilot/evidence/intent-gate/evidence/intent-fail-detail.png)

**Summary — 3 tests, 3 failures, 0% successful:**

![failing summary](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/galuis116/touchpilot/evidence/intent-gate/evidence/intent-fail-index.png)
